name: Size-to-scale visualization
logic: '12 oz' or 'Ø90 mm' is a number, not a mental image — and a buyer who can't picture the size hesitates or mis-orders. Showing the whole family to scale, or a product against a familiar reference, gives the instant physical grasp they'd get in a showroom. Baymard found 42% of users actively try to judge a product's size from its images, yet 28% of major retailers give them nothing to scale against — an own goal you fix with a single rendered lineup.

evidence.title: Product Page UX: Provide at Least One 'In Scale' Image
evidence.note: Verified 2026-06-11: '42% of users will attempt to gauge the overall scale and size of a product from its product images'; '28% of sites do not provide any In Scale images' across 60 major retailers.

exemplars.name: IKEA
exemplars.why: Dimension overlays and to-scale room context are core to every product page — buyers never guess size.

metricsNote: 42% of users try to judge product size from images (Baymard, verified).
